ingredients.name: Yakiniku Dining Taijuen Shimokitazawa
ingredients.localName: 焼肉ダイニング 太樹苑 下北沢店
ingredients.address: ＮＳビル, 2 Chome-8-8 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Seaside Line Shimohiroba
ingredients.localName: 金沢シーサイドライン下広場
ingredients.address: 10番 Uminokoen, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0013, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: A station on the Kanazawa Seaside Line monorail that serves Bocchi's home area in Kanazawa-ku, Yokohama — part of the distinctive transit infrastructure the anime uses to characterise her long commute from home to STARRY.

ingredients.name: KUROSAWA MUSIC Ochanomizu Station Front
ingredients.localName: クロサワ楽器 お茶の水駅前店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-2-2 Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: One of Ochanomizu's famous instrument shops that appears in Episode 12 when Bocchi goes to buy a new guitar — the music instrument district of Ochanomizu is where Seika Ijichi earned the nickname 'Satan of Ochanomizu' before opening STARRY.

ingredients.name: Spice Curry & Cafe SHANTi
ingredients.localName: Spice Curry & Cafe SHANTi 下北店
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒155-0031 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−12−2 Southwave Shimokitazawa 1F

ingredients.name: Times Daizawa No. 5
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-26-26 Daizawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: One of the photo shoot backdrops in Episode 4's artist photo session — the fence of the Daizawa car park features in the band's street-style shots.

ingredients.name: Burger King - Shimo-Kitazawa Station South Entrance
ingredients.localName: バーガーキング 下北沢駅南口店
ingredients.type: ファースト フード店
categories: ファースト フード店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-13-7 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Shimokitazawa Parking Lot
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-32-32 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Katase-Enoshima Station
ingredients.address: Kanagawa, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The station where Kessoku Band arrives for their Enoshima day trip in Episode 9 'Enoshima Escar' — the four members gathering out front is one of the series' most iconic shots. The distinctive façade is reproduced exactly in the anime.

ingredients.name: Couple Stairs Enoshima
ingredients.address: 江ノ島海岸（バス, 1 Chome-15-15 Katasekaigan, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The stone stairs on Enoshima island that the band climbs during the Episode 9 'Enoshima Escar' day trip — part of the island route connecting the torii gate to the shrines and Sea Candle.

ingredients.name: Enoshima esker 3rd section end point
ingredients.localName: 江の島エスカー3区終点
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-19 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor escalators (Enoshima Escar) that give Episode 9 its name — the third section's end point is the elevated plateau where the band emerges from the ascent with the sea view opening up. The most Bocchi-specific location in Enoshima.

ingredients.name: Village Vanguard Shimokitazawa
ingredients.localName: ヴィレッジヴァンガード下北沢店
ingredients.address: マルシェ下北沢 1F, 2 Chome-10-15 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In front of Village Vanguard is where Bocchi walks with Nijika after first arriving in Shimokitazawa (Ep 1), and where Bocchi invites Kita Ikuyo to join the band (Ep 3) — one of the most-visited street corners on the Bocchi pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Eshima Shrine Hoanden
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒251-0036 Kanagawa, Fujisawa, Enoshima, 2 Chome−3
ingredients.entityContext: Part of the Enoshima Shrine complex visited by Kessoku Band in Episode 9 — the shrine dedicated to the goddess of music that Kita specifically wanted to visit as a musician.

ingredients.name: Marucho
ingredients.address: 5 Chome-6-1 Daizawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Chinese restaurant in Shimokitazawa where Ryo works during the T-shirt design session in Episode 7, designing artwork for the band while on the job.

ingredients.name: Near the Cuore Forte
ingredients.localName: クオーレ・フォルテ付近
ingredients.address: 大成ビル 1階, 3 Chome-20-2 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Asahi Honten
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-8 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan

ingredients.name: Near the Trust Park Daizawa 2-Chome
ingredients.localName: トラストパーク 代沢2丁目付近
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-33-6 Daizawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0032, Japan

ingredients.name: Shimokita Senrogai Open Space
ingredients.localName: 下北線路街 空き地
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒155-0031 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−33−３３番１２号 付近, 下北沢交番の隣
ingredients.entityContext: The train yard open space where Nijika stands alone after the argument with her older sister Seika in Episode 5 — one of the series' quieter, more character-focused scenes outside the live house.

ingredients.name: Ishibashi Music - Ochanomizu Main Shop
ingredients.localName: イシバシ楽器 御茶ノ水本店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-２ Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The landmark Ishibashi Music shop in Ochanomizu's instrument district where the band heads in Episode 12 — Bocchi famously enters the store headbanging, flustered about having to talk to the clerk, and ends up buying a Yamaha Pacifica for ¥150,000.

ingredients.name: Donguri Hiroba Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-26-18 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small park that serves as one of the shooting locations in Kessoku Band's Episode 4 artist photo sequence — part of the Shimokitazawa backstreet tour the band goes on for their band photos.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Kaigan
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒251-0035 Kanagawa, Fujisawa, Katasekaigan, 1-chōme−15
ingredients.entityContext: The Enoshima coastline where the band spends time during the Episode 9 beach day — the seaside setting for the kite-attack scene where Bocchi loses her ice cream to Enoshima's notorious food-stealing birds.

ingredients.name: Grand Torii of Enoshima Jinja Shrine
ingredients.localName: 江島神社 朱の鳥居(大鳥居)
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-2 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The red torii gate marking the entrance to Enoshima Shrine — and per pilgrimage guides, the spot on the long approach where Bocchi first meets Kikuri Hiroi. The gateway to the island's shrine complex in Episode 9.

ingredients.name: JET SET Shimokitazawa
ingredients.localName: ジェットセット 下北沢店
ingredients.address: 柳川ビル 201号, 2 Chome-33-12 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Shimo-Kitazawa Station
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-24-2 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The station Bocchi arrives at in Episode 1 when Nijika brings her to the district for the first time — and the constant hub for the band throughout the series. The station plaza is used in the artist photo shoot (Ep 4), band gatherings, and several key transitional scenes.

ingredients.name: Street Performance Space
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan

ingredients.name: Harumiya
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-4-7 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan

ingredients.name: Near the Geki ko gekijo
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-6-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The alley beside the Geki small theater appears in Bocchi's first walk through Shimokitazawa with Nijika (Ep 1) and the street where Kita and Nijika reunite (Ep 3) — a narrow lane that recurs throughout the series as the band's neighbourhood shortcut.

ingredients.name: Yakiniku Restaurant YAZAWA Shimokitazawa
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-10-12 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Shelter Shimokitazawa
ingredients.address: 仙田商会仙田ビル B1, 2 Chome-6-10 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The real live house that is the near-identical model for STARRY — same basement staircase, same red-blue neon entrance, same intimate stage. Multiple Kessoku Band performances throughout the series happen here. The single most important Bocchi the Rock! pilgrimage spot.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Shrine - Hetsunomiya
ingredients.localName: 江島神社 辺津宮（へつのみや）
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-8 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Enoshima Shrine dedicated to Benzaiten, goddess of music and performing arts — the specific shrine Kita requests the band visit in Episode 9, making it a meaningful stop for a band about aspiring musicians.

ingredients.name: Kanazawahakkeieki Bicycle Parking
ingredients.localName: 金沢八景駅自転車駐車場
ingredients.address: 横浜市営駐輪場 金沢八景駅, 4-26 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The bicycle parking at Kanazawa-Hakkei Station that appears in the Episode 6–7 Hakkei neighbourhood sequences — a small, grounding detail from Bocchi's home commute area.

ingredients.name: Karaagekappofukunokara Shimokitazawaten
ingredients.localName: 福のから 下北沢店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-10-18 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Hakkei Bridge
ingredients.address: Fukazawa Komagome, Aomori, 030-0955, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge in the Kanazawa Hakkei area that appears in Episode 7 'To Your House' when the rest of the band travels to Bocchi's home neighbourhood — part of the coastal Yokohama scenery around her house.

ingredients.name: ADRIFT
ingredients.address: 〒155-0031 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 3 Chome−9−23 ADRIFT
ingredients.entityContext: The Shimokitazawa spot where Ryo and Bocchi part ways at the end of Episode 4 after the photo shoot and a day together — a brief but atmospheric farewell scene in the neighbourhood's quieter alleys.

ingredients.name: CREA BASE Shimokitazawa
ingredients.address: Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Hirose Photo Shop
ingredients.localName: ヒロセフォトショップ
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒155-0031 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−6−8, 広瀬ビル1F

ingredients.name: Shinjuku LOFT
ingredients.address: タテハナビル, 1 Chome-12-9 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The flagship live house that is the direct model for 'Shinjuku FOLT' in Episode 10 'After Dark' — where Kessoku Band shares a bill with Kikuri Hiroi's SICK HACK. The floor layout and stage position are reproduced almost exactly.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Sea Candle
ingredients.localName: 江の島シーキャンドル
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-28 Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The lighthouse observation tower at the top of Enoshima that appears in the Episode 9 day-trip sequence — offering panoramic views of the coastline and, on clear days, Mount Fuji.

ingredients.name: Focus
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒111-0051 Tokyo, Taito City, Kuramae, 4 Chome−21−2
ingredients.entityContext: The former 'Focus Shimokita' site that appears in the Episode 4 photo shoot sequence — one of the Shimokitazawa alley locations used for the band's artist photos.

ingredients.name: Biwajima Shrine
ingredients.address: 2 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The shrine on Biwajima in Kanazawa Hakkei, just east of the station, that appears in the Episode 6–7 Hakkei sequences — and the setting for Bocchi's first encounter with Kikuri Hiroi along the shrine's torii approach.

ingredients.name: 46ma
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-7-2 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Kokera
ingredients.address: 藤友ビル 1F, 2 Chome-2-13 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The seafood izakaya where the band gathers for a post-performance celebration in Episode 8 — one of the series' most relaxed group scenes as the members bond over food after a show.

ingredients.name: Shimokitazawa North Bicycle Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 下北沢北自転車等置場
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-19-46番先 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Kanazawa-Hakkei Station
ingredients.address: 15-1 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Bocchi's home station in Kanazawa-ku, Yokohama — the coastal area near her house that features prominently in Episodes 6 and 7. The commute from here to STARRY in Shimokitazawa takes Bocchi two hours each way.

ingredients.name: Hakkei Florist
ingredients.address: 15-15-1 Seto, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 236-0027, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: A florist in the Kanazawa Hakkei area that appears as background in the Episode 6–7 sequences set in Bocchi's home neighbourhood — part of the coastal Yokohama streetscape the show recreates.

ingredients.name: Toriyoshi
ingredients.address: Yajima Bldg., 1F, 2 Chome-8-6 Kamimeguro, Meguro City, Tokyo 153-0051, Japan

ingredients.name: Mos Burger Shimo-Kitazawa Kitaguchi
ingredients.localName: モスバーガー下北沢北口店
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-31-12 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The MOS Burger at Shimokitazawa's north exit appears in the Episode 4 photo shoot route — the 'in front of MOS Burger North Exit' stop is one of the band's documented photo locations.

ingredients.name: Sukiya (Shimokitazawa)
ingredients.address: 大町ビル, 2 Chome-12-7 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The gyudon chain near Shimokitazawa where a street scene between Bocchi and Kita takes place in Episode 10 — part of the dense Shimokitazawa streetscape the series maps with precision.

ingredients.name: Yanagi Chonai Kaikan
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒236-0026 Kanagawa, Yokohama, Kanazawa Ward, Yanagicho, 4-9
ingredients.entityContext: The Yanagi neighbourhood hall in Kanazawa Hakkei confirmed as a location in Episode 7 'To Your House' — part of the residential streetscape around Bocchi's home that the band discovers when they visit her.

ingredients.name: Times Shimo-Kitazawa No.8 Car Park
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-9 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The parking lot that serves as the final photo location in the Kessoku Band artist photo shoot sequence in Episode 4 — the last stop on an episode-long tour of Shimokitazawa backstreets.

ingredients.name: Suginami Ward Matsunoki Children's Park
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒166-0014 Tokyo, Suginami City, Matsunoki, 3 Chome−30−番８号
ingredients.entityContext: The modest children's park in Suginami where Nijika Ijichi asks Hitori Gotoh to play support guitar in Episode 1 — the exact moment Kessoku Band is born. Every pilgrimage guide marks this as the origin point of the story.
